methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $8.6B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Wayve
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $8.6B

private.name: Cohere
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $7B

private.name: Hugging Face
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $4.5B

private.name: Labelbox
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Hive AI is a Series D-stage AI & Software company founded by Kevin Guo, currently valued at $2.0B post-money with $120M+ raised to date. The peer set comprises four same-sector, same-stage comparables spanning $1.0B to $8.6B in valuation: Wayve ($8.6B), Cohere ($7.0B), Hugging Face ($4.5B), and Labelbox ($1.0B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier to the $2.0B last post-money, implying $3.0B. Peer-median analysis across the four comparables yields a median valuation of $5.8B, with a range floor of $1.0B and ceiling of $8.6B. The substantial spread reflects heterogeneity in business model and scale within the frontier AI sector at this stage.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $2.5B (low) to $6.6B (high), with a median of $4.4B. The current $2.0B valuation sits below this range, suggesting potential upside at Series E or later financing. Key uncertainties include revenue visibility and unit economics relative to the peer set, and the degree to which Hive AI's technology differentiation and market position track the higher-valued comparables like Wayve and Cohere versus lower-valued peers like Labelbox.
